textDisplay: The explanation for the second one. The old man fixing the generator seemingly indoors and dying is replaced with Reuben (the hospice worker who promised Sam pizza) fixing the generator outdoors and dying. Deleted scenes do often make it into horror film trailers, and I find it funny that people are only pointing them out for this film.
textOriginal: The explanation for the second one. The old man fixing the generator seemingly indoors and dying is replaced with Reuben (the hospice worker who promised Sam pizza) fixing the generator outdoors and dying. Deleted scenes do often make it into horror film trailers, and I find it funny that people are only pointing them out for this film.

Just watched this movie few hours ago. I give it 6.5 / 10. The first movie is still a masterpiece.

Don't get me wrong, It's still an enjoyable movie to watch, this movie is not boring at all. I like the acting, But It still lacks in something, It's feels like eating a pizza without tomato sauce or cheese.

If only they give a longer scene of interaction between the military and the monster, this movie could be at 8 / 10 or 10 / 10.
textOriginal:
Just watched this movie few hours ago. I give it 6.5 / 10. The first movie is still a masterpiece.

Don't get me wrong, It's still an enjoyable movie to watch, this movie is not boring at all. I like the acting, But It still lacks in something, It's feels like eating a pizza without tomato sauce or cheese.

If only they give a longer scene of interaction between the military and the monster, this movie could be at 8 / 10 or 10 / 10.

textDisplay: @Bruhloonski Society has never had problems with black characters, the problem is when already established characters change. Black people exist and it is normal for there to be stories with them. Bad Boys 4 was recently released and is a success, the Fallout series was launched with a black protagonist and was a success, several series and films with black protagonists have been and are still successful. The reason is that they are original stories with original characters. What makes you wrong is thinking that the reason is that the problem is that they are black. Being black is not the problem, the problem is that they cast black people to play roles they shouldn't play. Why are Black Panther, Bad Boy, Beverly Hills Cop successful? Because they are original. If you don't understand that the problem is not skin color but lack of originality, then you have room temperature IQ.

textDisplay: "In 'A Quiet Place: Day One,' New York's construction workers finally find a use for all those jackhammers: monster repellent! Who needs city planning when you've got survival instincts?"

textDisplay: "I hear 'A Quiet Place: Day One' turns New York City into the ultimate hide-and-seek battleground. Winner gets to live another day!"
textOriginal: "I hear 'A Quiet Place: Day One' turns New York City into the ultimate hide-and-seek battleground. Winner gets to live another day!"

textDisplay: "Who needs fireworks on the Fourth of July when you have explosions of silence in 'A Quiet Place: Day One'? Talk about a blast!"
textOriginal: "Who needs fireworks on the Fourth of July when you have explosions of silence in 'A Quiet Place: Day One'? Talk about a blast!"

textDisplay: "I hear 'A Quiet Place: Day One' is the ultimate ASMR experience. Forget about whispering into microphones; just watch New Yorkers tiptoe around and pray they don't become monster snacks!"
textOriginal: "I hear 'A Quiet Place: Day One' is the ultimate ASMR experience. Forget about whispering into microphones; just watch New Yorkers tiptoe around and pray they don't become monster snacks!"

textDisplay: "In 'A Quiet Place: Day One,' New York's real estate market finally finds its peace and quiet. No more noisy neighbors, just the occasional monster roaring down the block!"
textOriginal: "In 'A Quiet Place: Day One,' New York's real estate market finally finds its peace and quiet. No more noisy neighbors, just the occasional monster roaring down the block!"

textDisplay: "In the prequel to 'A Quiet Place,' we finally get to see how New Yorkers handle a situation without honking their horns every two seconds. Spoiler alert: it involves a lot of shushing!"
textOriginal: "In the prequel to 'A Quiet Place,' we finally get to see how New Yorkers handle a situation without honking their horns every two seconds. Spoiler alert: it involves a lot of shushing!"

textDisplay: "In 'A Quiet Place: Day One,' the streets of New York are so quiet, even the pigeons are tiptoeing around! Who knew a city that never sleeps could take a nap?"
textOriginal: "In 'A Quiet Place: Day One,' the streets of New York are so quiet, even the pigeons are tiptoeing around! Who knew a city that never sleeps could take a nap?"

textDisplay:
As a passionate 3D cinema enthusiast, I've converted the latest trailer for "A Quiet Place Day One" into high-quality 3D in just a few hours. This endeavor illustrates what individuals, armed with the right tools and a bit of dedication, can achieve. If someone like me can do this in a short time, imagine what large companies with substantially greater resources could accomplish.

The cost of producing in 3D has traditionally been high, but with advancements in AI technology, these costs should significantly decrease. It’s unreasonable for 3D in Hollywood to remain a pricey option when it could be made more accessible.

We need reasons to return to the cinemas, and maintaining the 3D format in immersive films like "A Quiet Place" could be crucial. 3D not only enhances the cinematic experience but also revitalizes the film industry by offering something that can’t be easily replicated at home. I hope the major studios as @Paramaunt Pictures recognize this and reconsider their approach to 3D, not just to keep it alive but to make it more affordable and spectacular than ever.

Trailer in 3D:

https://youtu.be/JX_Gc012dNw

textDisplay:
OK, I'm loving that this shows humans weren't totally dumb and it gives high hopes we can beat the aliens and rebuild civilization. 

The fact we see loads of people trying to orderly leave manhatten in silence, shows the government, military and general population realised early enough that silence keeps you safe. 

This means there's a high chance that there's a sizable population of humans left globally, all living in secured locations having made it to safe zones or that there are loads of rural communities standing. 

We've only ever seen the family from the main films who lived in a small town and went into hiding in the woods. So we've no idea how the other 8 billion humans reached. This tells me there are tens of millions if not hundreds of millions alive still in the "present day" in the franchise as well as intact governments and militaries. Just they have probably pulled back to protect remaining population centres and anyone left outside these zones (like the family in the other films) are on their own unless they find a safe zone themselves. Governments won't be broadcasting location by radio or sending out planes or vehicles to help people for obvious reasons. You either got lucky and ended up in a secure location in the first week or you go stuck to fend for yourself (or lived super isolated in mountains or on tiny islands and cut your tiny community off from the world).

This franchise is quickly becoming the best monster franchise in decades.
textOriginal:
OK, I'm loving that this shows humans weren't totally dumb and it gives high hopes we can beat the aliens and rebuild civilization. 

The fact we see loads of people trying to orderly leave manhatten in silence, shows the government, military and general population realised early enough that silence keeps you safe. 

This means there's a high chance that there's a sizable population of humans left globally, all living in secured locations having made it to safe zones or that there are loads of rural communities standing. 

We've only ever seen the family from the main films who lived in a small town and went into hiding in the woods. So we've no idea how the other 8 billion humans reached. This tells me there are tens of millions if not hundreds of millions alive still in the "present day" in the franchise as well as intact governments and militaries. Just they have probably pulled back to protect remaining population centres and anyone left outside these zones (like the family in the other films) are on their own unless they find a safe zone themselves. Governments won't be broadcasting location by radio or sending out planes or vehicles to help people for obvious reasons. You either got lucky and ended up in a secure location in the first week or you go stuck to fend for yourself (or lived super isolated in mountains or on tiny islands and cut your tiny community off from the world).

This franchise is quickly becoming the best monster franchise in decades.

textDisplay: Whenever global cataclysms occur, they seem to exclusively target the United States, as if no other nation matters. Only the American government steps up to tackle these crises, while the rest of the world appears ineffectual. Even in fictional scenarios like alien invasions, the focus is always on the US being under attack. These narratives lack depth, emotion, or any intriguing plot twists—they're just plain dull. It's high time for a fresh approach, a movie genre that breaks free from these tired tropes and explores something truly innovative and unconventional.
textOriginal: Whenever global cataclysms occur, they seem to exclusively target the United States, as if no other nation matters. Only the American government steps up to tackle these crises, while the rest of the world appears ineffectual. Even in fictional scenarios like alien invasions, the focus is always on the US being under attack. These narratives lack depth, emotion, or any intriguing plot twists—they're just plain dull. It's high time for a fresh approach, a movie genre that breaks free from these tired tropes and explores something truly innovative and unconventional.

textDisplay: Nossa, o trailer de cara já mostra tensão, mostrando o caos do primeiro dia da invasão alienígena. A sensação de perigo é constante e promete deixar todo mundo bem concentrado no filme, e grudado na cadeira. Os atores fizeram um ótimo papel, a química entre eles é chamativa e traz uma sensação emocional ao filme que vai conquistar os telespectadores. Eu como amantes de filmes n deixo de observar os efeitos visuais, e os efeitos desse filme ta bem chamativo e o design de som continua sendo um destaque no trailer. O som é uma parte importante em um trailer, e aqui eles usam isso de forma brilhante para aumentar ainda mais o suspense. Top🔥🤩

textDisplay:
Esto sin duda alguna 😅😮❤❤❤❤, será CINE 🎥🚬😍, una chulada de película para disfrutar en este 2024.
A QUIET PLACE:DAY ONE (2024) no me la 🧾😮❤ pienso perder para nada, se ve que esto estará tremendo, uyyyyy.
La historia del origen de la invasión alienígena 👽🤖🤡🤮 que acabó con la humanidad, es algo alucinante, que no me pienso perder.
Creo que puedo decir que Lupita Nyong'o 😢🎉😂❤ junto a Joseph Quinn como los protagonistas principales: Sam y Erick se lucen en cada escena.
Yo si me enojaría si el pobre gatito 😢🎉😂 no vive casi nada de todo el filme.
Hasta se nota 📝😅❤ que tendremos otro éxito de taquilla asegurado como las dos anteriores películas.
Ya quiero que llegue el gran momento el 28 de junio.
No puedo esperar.
😅😅😅😅😅😅😅😅.

textDisplay: I still refuse to believe that the full might of the world's military could fall to these creatures, seeing how they fare against conventional firearms in the first and second movie. Hopefully, this movie could properly explain how they did it, especially with those jets, gunships, and naval forces still around, and i do think that ultrasonic crowd control weapons exists irl so once they figure out that they're super sensitive to sound, i don't think it'll take a genius to notice the effectiveness of such weaponry.
textOriginal: I still refuse to believe that the full might of the world's military could fall to these creatures, seeing how they fare against conventional firearms in the first and second movie. Hopefully, this movie could properly explain how they did it, especially with those jets, gunships, and naval forces still around, and i do think that ultrasonic crowd control weapons exists irl so once they figure out that they're super sensitive to sound, i don't think it'll take a genius to notice the effectiveness of such weaponry.

textDisplay: Don't panic. We will survive because fortunately for us, every alien civilisation that invades us is mind numbingly stupid beyond belief. Think about it. These aliens have mastered intergalactic propulsion systems. They have mastered technologies to keep themselves alive while traversing the void of space from their galaxy to ours. They have mastered sensor technologies that allow them to accurately determine whether a planet is inhabited or not (that's how they found Earth, no?). All these technologies are still science fiction to us. That means these aliens are vastly superior to us that it's actually hilarious. They should possess weapons technologies that are beyond our wildest sci-fi fantasies, right? And yet they get here and start chasing us around the streets on foot so they can bite us or squash us under their feet. When they burst into a house, they can't tell when a human is hiding under a table even though they have sensors that can detect habitable planets from another galaxy. That makes absolutely no sense. So, like I said, don't panic. We will be fine.
textOriginal: Don't panic. We will survive because fortunately for us, every alien civilisation that invades us is mind numbingly stupid beyond belief. Think about it. These aliens have mastered intergalactic propulsion systems. They have mastered technologies to keep themselves alive while traversing the void of space from their galaxy to ours. They have mastered sensor technologies that allow them to accurately determine whether a planet is inhabited or not (that's how they found Earth, no?). All these technologies are still science fiction to us. That means these aliens are vastly superior to us that it's actually hilarious. They should possess weapons technologies that are beyond our wildest sci-fi fantasies, right? And yet they get here and start chasing us around the streets on foot so they can bite us or squash us under their feet. When they burst into a house, they can't tell when a human is hiding under a table even though they have sensors that can detect habitable planets from another galaxy. That makes absolutely no sense. So, like I said, don't panic. We will be fine.

textDisplay:
The weakest part of the movies`s plot is... the core of the plot -  those monsters who have extremely keen ears.  

If the creature can hear a tiny sound kilometers away, that means, the loud sound will definitely hurt it. The very loud sound will simply damage it`s hearing organs and, due to creatures` blindness, make them completely disabled. Did you ever hear the sound of tank engine in a close range? - I did. It is very loud . Same for the jets, ships, shots and explosions.

So all that ''battle'' between the aliens and men`s modern armies does not make any sense - aliens will lose before humans make their first shot,

textDisplay:
Don't get me wrong, love the 'A Quiet Place' stuff but I hope some film addresses the fact if a waterfall confuses the creatures why wouldn't a string of windchimes / radios / static noise etc be able to create instant sound camo anywhere?  Seems like people / military would have figured that stuff out pretty quick.  Also since the creatures basically tackle any source of noise then improvised traps (wall of spikes, large falls, fire) would be super effective against them.

Not to mention why would people occupy houses, small buildings, and so on.  Larger buildings with many layers of walls / doors would be much safer to live in vs a random farm house like in the first movie

Great concept...just very poorly thought out.
textOriginal:
Don't get me wrong, love the 'A Quiet Place' stuff but I hope some film addresses the fact if a waterfall confuses the creatures why wouldn't a string of windchimes / radios / static noise etc be able to create instant sound camo anywhere?  Seems like people / military would have figured that stuff out pretty quick.  Also since the creatures basically tackle any source of noise then improvised traps (wall of spikes, large falls, fire) would be super effective against them.

Not to mention why would people occupy houses, small buildings, and so on.  Larger buildings with many layers of walls / doors would be much safer to live in vs a random farm house like in the first movie

Great concept...just very poorly thought out.

textDisplay: Waterfall doesn't confuse them, it just mask other sounds. There's a specific frequency paired with the hearing aid and radio that expose the aliens weak spot. Other sounds like chimes can just be used as a distraction but it's risky.
textOriginal: Waterfall doesn't confuse them, it just mask other sounds. There's a specific frequency paired with the hearing aid and radio that expose the aliens weak spot. Other sounds like chimes can just be used as a distraction but it's risky.

textDisplay: Perhaps the stupidest premise in sci-fi entertainment history.  And that is saying something.  P.S. For those who do not know, being deaf does not make one more quiet.  It means you don't actually know how much noise you are making, and thus your default is  noisy. P.P.S.: Cats are not quiet on command and make a ton of noise when wishing to communicate.
textOriginal: Perhaps the stupidest premise in sci-fi entertainment history.  And that is saying something.  P.S. For those who do not know, being deaf does not make one more quiet.  It means you don't actually know how much noise you are making, and thus your default is  noisy. P.P.S.: Cats are not quiet on command and make a ton of noise when wishing to communicate.

textDisplay:
@kisumufilms1495 Also:
"The actor vs. actress debate emerged in step with the Women’s Liberation Movement of the 1960s, 70s, and 80s. The continued distinction between males and females who act started being viewed as not only unnecessary but also demeaning toward women performing the same job as men. Cate Blanchett has said, “I have always referred to myself as an actor. I am of the generation where the word actress was used almost always in a pejorative sense. So I claim the other space.” Similarly, Whoopi Goldberg told The Guardian: “An actress can only play a woman. I'm an actor—I can play anything.” "
textOriginal:
@kisumufilms1495 Also:
"The actor vs. actress debate emerged in step with the Women’s Liberation Movement of the 1960s, 70s, and 80s. The continued distinction between males and females who act started being viewed as not only unnecessary but also demeaning toward women performing the same job as men. Cate Blanchett has said, “I have always referred to myself as an actor. I am of the generation where the word actress was used almost always in a pejorative sense. So I claim the other space.” Similarly, Whoopi Goldberg told The Guardian: “An actress can only play a woman. I'm an actor—I can play anything.” "
